wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The individuals share the same name, but their occupations are fundamentally different. The card describes an electrical engineer working for a specific instrument company in Mount Vernon, NY, whereas the Wikidata entity describes an economist with a history of doctoral students and academic advisors.

results.possibleIdentity: Edward Greenberg was an electrical engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, based in Brooklyn, New York. He was employed by the Electrical Instrument Service Company (also known as Electrical Instrument Service, Inc.), located at 25 Dock Street, Mount Vernon, New York. His technical expertise was in the field of electronic measuring instruments. During this period, the Electrical Instrument Service Company served as an authorized service station for B&K Precision test equipment and performed technical work for government agencies, including developing calibration techniques for lightning protection testing in aircraft systems. Greenberg is associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, a non-profit group founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. He likely participated in E.A.T.'s technical services program, where engineers provided specialized knowledge to assist artists with new media projects.
